audit_items.proof_trace: sort.c uses Redis reference counting consistently across all code paths. `lookupKeyByPattern` frees `keyobj` and `fieldobj` exactly once on both the success path (L125-126) and the noobj path (L130-131), with `fieldlen>0 iff fieldobj!=NULL` ensuring equivalence. `sortCommand` protects `sortval` with `incrRefCount` before any mutation, and the cmpobj-aliasing case (when sortby="#" and alpha=true) is safe: `lookupKeyByPattern` increments refcount once and `getDecodedObject` increments it again, with two matching cleanup decrements at L581 and L587 — the object is freed only at the last decrement with no subsequent access.
